items.title: Tens of thousands make their way to devastated northern Gaza | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Tens of thousands of Palestinians are returning to their homes in northern Gaza for the first time in more than a year. Israel lifted restrictions on access after resolving a dispute with Hamas over the latest hostage release. Many are making the journey on foot, anxious to reunite with their families.

00:00 Mass exodus to northern Gaza
02:17 DW's Emily Gordine reports from Jerusalem
05:38 DW speaks with Amjad Shawa, head of the Palestinian NGO Network

items.title: 80th anniversary of Auschwitz liberation marked by survivors and world leaders | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>World leaders and Holocaust survivors gathered in the Polish town of Oswiecim to mark the 80th anniversary of the liberation of the Nazi death camp Auschwitz-Birkenau. 
The ceremony focussed on testimonies of surviving former inmates. Some wore blue and white scarves, the colors worn by the death camp prisoners. 
Several international leaders attended the event including German Chancellor Olaf Scholz, French President Emmanuel Macron and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. 
Auschwitz was the largest of the extermination camps and has become a symbol of Nazi Germany's genocide of six million Jews. Around 1.1 million people, the majority Jewish, were murdered at the site in German-occupied Poland between 1940 and 1945. 

#auschwitz #holocaust #shoah
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: German conservative candidate Merz to demolish far-right 'firewall'? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>German opposition leader Friedrich Merz says he'll push forward with a vote in parliament on tough new immigration legislation, possibly with help from the far right. 
The conservative is the frontrunner to become Germany's next chancellor after February's elections. Merz wants to bring back permanent controls on German borders and turn away migrants without proper documentation.

items.title: Eighty years after Holocaust: Jewish life in Germany still faces threats | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Today marks 80 years since the liberation of the Auschwitz death camp, recognized in Germany as a nationwide day of remembrance for the victims of the Nazi regime. But even now, Germany’s synagogues and Jewish institutions still require police protection due to antisemitic attacks and threats. The prospect of the far-right AfD performing strongly in German elections next month have only added to the sense of unease. 

00:00 Jewish communities seek to build bridges in Germany
04:20 Dalia Grinfeld, Anti-Defamation League



#auschwitz #ww2 #holocaust

items.title: Israel continues raids in occupied West Bank | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israel launched a series of raids in the occupied West Bank soon after the ceasefire in Gaza between Hamas and Israel took effect. At least 12 Palestinians have been killed so far, the UN said, and many more have been forced to evacuate their homes. Israel's military says it is searching for Iran-backed militants in the Jenin refugee camp, a longtime flashpoint in the conflict.

items.title: M23 rebels in DR Congo say they have captured Goma | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>M23 rebels in the Democratic Republic of Congo say they have captured the eastern city of Goma, after days of heavy fighting with government forces and UN peacekeepers. 
The M23 militia is backed by neighboring Rwanda, and the United Nations has warned of a major humanitarian crisis. Thousands of civilians have already fled across the border into Rwanda. There are reports that troops from the two neighbouring countries have been firing at each other across the border. Goma is the biggest city in eastern Congo - home to around 1.7 million residents and displaced people.

00:00 M23 claims capture of Goma
02:47 Journalist Al Katanty Djaffar reports from Goma
06:55 DW speaks with Myriam Favier, head of the International Red Cross's subdelegation in Goma

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Israel lets Palestinians return to northern Gaza: What will they find? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Thousands of displaced Palestinians are now crossing into northern Gaza for the first time in over a year. Israel has agreed to allow their passage as part of the ceasefire deal with the militant Hamas group. 
Gazans began returning on Monday morning after waiting days to cross. Their return had been delayed after Israel blocked their passage, accusing Hamas of breaching the terms of the truce by not freeing civilian hostages first. The militants have agreed to release more Israeli captives this week in exchange.

items.title: EU cautiously weighs lifting sanctions on Syria, with caveats | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Calls are mounting for Western nations to lift sanctions on Syria after former President Bashar Al-Assad was overthrown by rebel groups last month. European countries have begun engaging with the new interim authorities, but the EU remains hesitant to lift sanctions before it sees concrete changes in Syria. The issue is once again on the table at a gathering of EU foreign ministers in Brussels.

items.title: HIV breakthrough drug priced out of reach for those who need it most | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The UN agency leading the world's response to HIV and AIDS, is making an urgent call to action: UNAIDS wants a groundbreaking HIV prevention drug to be made available to everyone who needs it. The drug, Lenacapavir, requires just two injections a year and has shown remarkable success in trials. 
But there's a problem: it currently costs each patient more than forty thousand dollars per year - that's far too expensive for most.

items.title: Has Lukashenko silenced the Belarusian opposition? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Exit polls in Belarus indicate that longtime ruler Alexander Lukashenko has won the country's presidential election. As polls close there, election authorities are already reporting that Lukashenko has captured 87-point-6 percent of the vote. Lukashenko - who has close ties to Moscow -  has now been in power since 1994. Election observers say the vote was largely orchestrated - rigged - and that any meaningful opponents are in prison or exiled.

items.title: How Arab countries are responding to Trump's 'clean out' Gaza remarks to expel Palestinians
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>On Saturday, US President Trump suggested that Palestinians be expelled from the Gaza Strip to "clean out" the territory. Trump called the Gaza Strip a "demolition site" and that ethnically cleansing Palestinians from the enclave could be a temporary or a long-term arrangement. President Trump said he had spoken to Jordanian King Abdullah about Jordan taking in Palestinians, and that he would speak to Egyptian President el-Sissi on Sunday for Egypt to do the same. Trump's "clean out" remarks have stirred controversy, as any forced expulsion of Palestinians would violate international law and jeopardize the creation of a Palestinian state.  

Chapter Breakdown: 
0:00 Palestinian Islamic Jihad announces release of Arbel Yehud
0:40 Joost Hiltermann, MENA Program Director at the International Crisis Group, on Trump's suggestion to 'clean out' Gaza and how Arab countries are responding

items.title: Palestinian Islamic Jihad announces release of Israeli civilian prior to next hostage-prisoner swap
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The militant group Palestinian Islamic Jihad in Gaza says it will free Israeli civilian hostage Arbel Yehud before the next planned swap where Israel is to release Palestinian prisoners and detainees in exchange for Hamas returning Israeli hostages. According to the terms of the Israel-Hamas ceasefire agreement, Arbel Yehud should have been handed over by Hamas this past Saturday. Hamas, however, delayed the release over what they called technical reasons. Israel has blocked tens of thousands of Palestinians from returning to their homes in northern Gaza, and says their return is conditional on Yehud's release.

DW speaks to journalist Balig Sladeen.

#arbelyehud
#israelihostages #palestinianislamicjihad

items.title: Israeli forces remain in southern Lebanon past withdrawal date, kill at least 22 Lebanese | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Tensions have erupted along the Lebanon-Israel border as Israeli forces remain in southern Lebanon past the agreed withdrawal deadline under a fragile ceasefire with Hezbollah. According to Lebanese health officials, at least 22 people were killed and over 80 injured when Israeli troops fired on residents attempting to return to their homes.  The deadly incident is likely to enflame the humanitarian crisis for the 112,000 displaced Lebanese.

items.title: Can the EU compete with China’s rail plans? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A deadly accident at a train station in Serbia has put a spotlight on China’s outsized role in the Balkans and Hungary, particularly its funding for large rail projects. Europe is now trying to keep up, but is it enough? DW looks at a geopolitical competition centered around rail, trade and the future orientation of a region on the EU’s periphery.

01:34   Albania – The race to modernize

items.title: Israeli troops kill civilians returning to southern Lebanon villages | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The Lebanese Health Ministry says at least two people are dead in southern Lebanon after Israeli soldiers fired on residents carrying Hezbollah flags. 
The ministry said another 31 people had been injured near the border with the Israeli-occupied Golan Heights. Tensions have been rising in the area as residents have been returning to their homes despite the presence of Israeli forces. The troops were set to withdraw by Sunday under a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah militants. But Israel said on Friday they would remain there longer.

00:00 Israeli soldiers fire on Lebanese villagers
00:42 Journalist Karim El-Gawhary reports from Kafr Kila in Southern Lebanon

items.title: Growing crackdown on dissent raises alarms in Uganda | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Fears are growing in Uganda of an escalating crackdown on dissent. In just the past few months, opposition leader Kizza Besigye has been charged with treason, his lawyer was imprisoned, and several activists have been arrested for criticizing the government online. DW correspondent Julius Mugambwa has more from Kampala, on what critics are calling a dangerous new phase of repression.

items.title: US issues broad freeze on foreign aid, excepting Israel and Egypt | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The Trump administration has issued a halt to nearly all existing foreign aid, with the exception of that flowing to Israel and Egypt. That's according to a State department memo seen by US media outlets. The freeze stems from an executive order signed by Trump within hours of taking office, ordering a 90 day pause in foreign development assisstance in order to review efficiency and consistency with his foreign policy.

items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Ukraine's President Volodymr Zelenskyy has dismissed comments from Russian President Vladimir Putin that he is ready for talks to end the war in Ukraine. Zelenksyy characterized Putin's comments  as "manipulation." Putin gave an interview to state TV, praising US President Trump. He also said he and Trump had a pragmatic and trusting relationship and suggested they should meet to discuss ending the Ukraine war.

Meanwhile there are media reports that the US is halting foreign aid to almost all countries for 90 days. According to a leaked internal memo from the US State department, only two countries -  Israel and Egypt are exempt from the freeze.

items.title: Why Russia and North Korea can't quit each other (for now) | Mapped Out
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Kim Jong Un and Vladimir Putin seem to be meeting each other's needs. Russia's leader reportedly has access to North Korean troops as well as ammunition for his full-scale war against Ukraine. And the North Korean leader has a backer who seems keen to evade international sanctions. The new bond could shift global geopolitics with China also in the mix. But what’s really driving Kim and Putin together: A shared vision, desperation, or something far more pragmatic?

items.title: Can Yunus master the transition in Bangladesh? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Will sectarian tensions overwhelm the new government of Bangladesh—Islamist parties repressed under the former regime now can pursue their ideology. Is the country's Hindu minority being targeted as some in India claim? Does the change of government open the door for a thaw in relations with Pakistan for the first time since indepdence and how would that be seen in New Delhi? 
  
Since former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina fled Bangladesh in August 2024 the country’s interim leader, Nobel laureate Muhammad Yunus has been confronted with mulple challenges. Hasina fled to India after 15 years in power and faces multiple investigations including for potential crimes against humanity.  

Is Bangladesh on the brink of chaos or can it master the transition to stability and democracy? This is the fourth episode of DW’s Inside Asian Conflicts series. 

#muhammadyunus #insideasianconflicts
#bangladeshindia

items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The United States has started conducting deportation flights using Air Force planes. As Customs and Border Protection confirmed to DW, a C-17 cargo plane, carrying 80 Guatemalan migrants, departed on Thursday evening. US President Donald Trump also ordered 1,500 more military personnel to the border with Mexico, like here on the Paso del Norte International Bridge. Since taking office, President Trump has signed a series of executive orders on migration.

Trump also left thousands of Afghans refugees in limbo after the suspension of a resettlement program.

items.title: Storm Eowyn slams UK following trail of destruction in Ireland | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Storm Eowyn has slammed into Scotland and Northern England, after leaving a trail of destruction across Ireland. 

The Republic of Ireland recorded wind gusts of 183 kilometres an hour -- its strongest on record. Eowyn left almost one million households and businesses in Ireland without power. Scotland and the North of England are also taking a battering from the storm, with rail services cancelled and schools closed out of safety concerns.

items.title: Can Friedrich Merz win over female voters? | Berlin Briefing Podcast
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Michaela Küfner, DW Chief Political Editor:
He himself recognises that the CDU for success needs women voters. But the younger the women are that get polled, the worse he does. Below 18, he's even below 9%. Well, you can say that doesn't really matter because they're not going to vote anyway. The voting age in Germany is 18, but above that it's not that terribly much more. And he just simply comes across as a man of the 90s. He's tone deaf to the issue of women's representation. Just as an example, in 2023, there were seven tickets for the Munich Security Conference for his party. He sent seven men. He doesn't have anyone who's very a close advisor to him who's female, his spokespeople, all men. Only the head of the Konrad Adenauer house of the party, that's a female speaker.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Who is Friedrich Merz, Germany's likely next leader? | Berlin Briefing Podcast
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>John Kampfner, Journalist and Author:
He's a man who, as you say, he's been in politics for a long time. He's acerbic. He doesn't suffer fools gladly. He's capable of being quite rude to people. He knows his mind. Both because of his relationship to Angela Merkel and feeling that she had stabbed him in the front and the back, and is very keen to do the same now to her during this election campaign and afterwards. He has committed what, for some traditional Germans think is a cardinal sin and worked in the private sector for the asset company Blackrock and sort of a big global company, which in the sort of millennial feeling of Germany's centre-left, is regarded as beyond the pale.

items.title: How Germany's super-rich stay super-rich | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The number of billionaires is rising rapidly worldwide, including in Germany. Despite an inheritance tax, wealth continues to grow untaxed for the ultra-wealthy. How is that possible? 

Ahead of the elections in Germany, most political parties overall promise tax relief in their election manifestos. While the Greens, SPD and BSW propose reforms to the inheritance tax aimed at generating an additional €3 billion annually, other parties advocate for reductions. The AfD's proposal could even slash annual inheritance tax revenue in Germany by as much as €10 billion. These policies could widen the gap between the rich and the poor. 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: How Russia is attempting to undermine this eastern European nation | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Moldova's prime minister accuses Russia of causing a humanitarian crisis in the breakaway state of Transnistria. The territory is internationally recognized as part of Moldova, but Russia has long stationed troops in the narrow strip of land that borders Ukraine. In this interview with DW Editor-in-Chief, Manuela Kasper-Claridge, Moldovan PM Dorin Recean explains how Russia is attempting to put a wedge between Transnistria and the Moldovan government, at the cost of the people living there.

items.title: Friedrich Merz: What to expect from Germany’s likely next leader | Berlin Briefing
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Friedrich Merz is the frontrunner to become Germany’s next leader after February’s elections. If he succeeds, he’ll face huge challenges: restoring economic growth after two years of recession, and delivering political stability after the infighting of the outgoing coalition. Experts warn that the stakes for a Merz-led government will be high: failure to deliver could fuel the far right. In this edition of Berlin Briefing, DW’s Richard Walker and Michaela Küfner break it all down with writer John Kampfner. Who is Friedrich Merz? Why did he have a feud with Angela Merkel? How would he deal with Donald Trump, Vladimir Putin, the Ukraine war and the challenge of China? And can he deliver the reboot that he says Germany needs?

Links:

Michaela Küfner on Friedrich Merz vs Olaf Scholz: https://youtu.be/WaBvj_GqbRE?feature=shared

John Kampfner: https://www.jkampfner.net
